meta.title: Kbd
meta.description: The KBD component visually renders a keystroke or key combination to highlight it within textual instructions.

mdx:
## Overview The keyboard key component provides semantic and visual representation for keyboard input, such as a shortcut, key press, or command line input, ensuring clarity and consistency when providing user instructions. ## Variables Get familiar with the features. ### Basic ```jsx live const App = () => Ctrl ``` ### Key combinations ```jsx live const App = () =>
Press Ctrl + C to copy
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory, cognitive, and physical limitations. ```jsx live const App = () =>
Press Ctrl + C to copy
``` ### Accessibility standards - **Semantic meaning:** The `` element provides clear semantic meaning to screen readers, indicating that the enclosed text represents keyboard input. - **Reading flow:** Ensure the KBD component doesn't interrupt the natural reading flow of the surrounding sentence. - **Contrast:** The key's background color and the text color must adhere to WCAG contrast guidelines for legibility. - **Focus (N/A):** Note that the KBD component itself is generally non-interactive and therefore should not be focusable via the keyboard.

dev.mdx:
## Getting started

### Import

```tsx
import { Kbd } from '@commercetools/nimbus';
```

### Basic usage

The Kbd component displays keyboard keys and shortcuts with consistent styling:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Text>
    Press <Kbd>Enter</Kbd> to submit the form.
  </Text>
)
```

## Usage examples

### Single keys

Display individual keyboard keys:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="300">
    <Text>Press <Kbd>A</Kbd> to select all</Text>
    <Text>Press <Kbd>Esc</Kbd> to cancel</Text>
    <Text>Press <Kbd>Tab</Kbd> to move focus</Text>
    <Text>Press <Kbd>Space</Kbd> to activate</Text>
    <Text>Press <Kbd>Delete</Kbd> to remove</Text>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Key combinations

Display multiple keys pressed together using separate Kbd components:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="300">
    <Text>
      Press <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>C</Kbd> to copy
    </Text>
    <Text>
      Press <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>V</Kbd> to paste
    </Text>
    <Text>
      Press <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>Z</Kbd> to undo
    </Text>
    <Text>
      Press <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>Shift</Kbd> + <Kbd>Z</Kbd> to redo
    </Text>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Modifier keys

Show common modifier key combinations:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="300">
    <Text>
      <Kbd>⌘</Kbd> + <Kbd>S</Kbd> to save (macOS)
    </Text>
    <Text>
      <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>S</Kbd> to save (Windows/Linux)
    </Text>
    <Text>
      <Kbd>Alt</Kbd> + <Kbd>Tab</Kbd> to switch windows
    </Text>
    <Text>
      <Kbd>Shift</Kbd> + <Kbd>Enter</Kbd> to insert line break
    </Text>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Arrow keys

Display directional navigation keys:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="300">
    <Text>Use <Kbd>↑</Kbd> and <Kbd>↓</Kbd> to navigate options</Text>
    <Text>Use <Kbd>←</Kbd> and <Kbd>→</Kbd> to move cursor</Text>
    <Text>
      Press <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>↑</Kbd> to scroll to top
    </Text>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Function keys

Display function keys for special actions:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="300">
    <Text>Press <Kbd>F1</Kbd> for help</Text>
    <Text>Press <Kbd>F5</Kbd> to refresh</Text>
    <Text>Press <Kbd>F11</Kbd> for fullscreen</Text>
    <Text>Press <Kbd>F12</Kbd> to open developer tools</Text>
  </Stack>
)
```

## Component requirements

## Accessibility

The Kbd component renders semantic `<kbd>` HTML elements which are recognized by screen readers as keyboard input. This helps assistive technology users understand that the content represents keys to press.

**Best practices:**
- Always use Kbd within descriptive text that explains the action
- Provide clear context about what the keyboard shortcut does
- Use consistent patterns for key combinations (e.g., modifier + key)
- Consider providing alternative methods for users who cannot use keyboards

**Example with clear context:**
```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Text>
    To save your changes, press <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>S</Kbd> or use the Save button.
  </Text>
)
```

#### Keyboard navigation

The Kbd component does not have interactive elements and does not require keyboard navigation. It is a display-only component used to visually represent keyboard keys.

## API reference

<PropsTable id="Kbd" />

## Common patterns

### Help dialog shortcuts

Display available keyboard shortcuts in a help menu:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
    <Text fontWeight="semibold">Keyboard Shortcuts</Text>
    <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
      <Stack direction="row" justify="space-between" width="300px">
        <Text>Copy</Text>
        <Stack direction="row" gap="100" align="center">
          <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd>
          <Text>+</Text>
          <Kbd>C</Kbd>
        </Stack>
      </Stack>
      <Stack direction="row" justify="space-between" width="300px">
        <Text>Paste</Text>
        <Stack direction="row" gap="100" align="center">
          <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd>
          <Text>+</Text>
          <Kbd>V</Kbd>
        </Stack>
      </Stack>
      <Stack direction="row" justify="space-between" width="300px">
        <Text>Search</Text>
        <Stack direction="row" gap="100" align="center">
          <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd>
          <Text>+</Text>
          <Kbd>F</Kbd>
        </Stack>
      </Stack>
    </Stack>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Form field hints

Show keyboard shortcuts in form field descriptions:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="300">
    <Text>
      Type to search. Press <Kbd>Enter</Kbd> to select the first result.
    </Text>
    <Text>
      Use <Kbd>Tab</Kbd> to move to the next field, or <Kbd>Shift</Kbd> + <Kbd>Tab</Kbd> to go back.
    </Text>
    <Text>
      Press <Kbd>Esc</Kbd> to clear your input and close suggestions.
    </Text>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Editor shortcuts

Display text editor keyboard commands:

```jsx live-dev
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="300">
    <Text fontWeight="semibold">Text Formatting</Text>
    <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
      <Text>
        <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>B</Kbd> for bold
      </Text>
      <Text>
        <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>I</Kbd> for italic
      </Text>
      <Text>
        <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>U</Kbd> for underline
      </Text>
      <Text>
        <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd> + <Kbd>K</Kbd> to insert link
      </Text>
    </Stack>
  </Stack>
)
```

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Visually displaying keyboard components helps users learn how to work more
quickly and more efficiently.

### Best practices

- **Contextual usage:** Only use the KBD component when referencing a literal
  keyboard key press or shortcut. Do not use it for general UI elements (like
  buttons or links).
- **Maintain conciseness:** Keep the content inside the KBD component
  short—usually one or two words, or a symbol—to represent the specific key
  (e.g., Enter, Shift, Ctrl).
- **Combinations:** Clearly indicate key combinations using a simple, recognized
  separator (e.g., + or a hyphen) within the KBD component (e.g., Ctrl + S).
- **Visual consistency:** Ensure the KBD component's border, background color,
  and font style make it visually distinct from the surrounding text while
  remaining integrated with the overall design.
- **Appropriate sizing:** The size of the KBD text and box should be
  proportional to the surrounding body text to avoid unnecessary visual
  distraction.

### Usage

This component is an additional way to convey information in a non interactive
way. Use in moderation, using too often can make text unreadable.

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- Displaying keyboard shortcuts in help text or tooltips
- Representing individual keys in user interfaces
- Highlighting keyboard navigation instructions
- To display command line input or terminal code within technical instructions.
- To clearly show keyboard shortcuts in application documentation or tooltips
  (e.g., "Press Ctrl + Z to undo").

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- Don't allow the component to wrap excessively; keep the contained text short.
- Don't use KBD for displaying long code snippets (use a `<code>` block
  instead).
- Don't use KBD without accompanying explanatory text to provide context.
